scores.correctness_notes: Covers the core concepts well (changes, tasks, statuses, polling vs blocking). Misses some gold standard details: no mention of CLI commands (pebble changes, pebble tasks), no mention of the REST API endpoints (GET /v1/changes/{id}, /wait, ?select=in-progress), no mention of change-update notices, and no mention of state persistence to $PEBBLE/.pebble.state. Focus is heavily ops/Python client side.
scores.hallucination_notes: container.pebble.start_services() returning a Change object is questionable — the gold standard says methods like stop_services() automatically wait. The change.err property and ChangeError usage pattern may not exactly match the actual ops API. container.pebble.wait_change() and get_change() may exist but are not confirmed in the gold standard. Minor risk of invented method signatures.
